perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction and for no other purpose, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for any purpose, including purposes beyond evaluating a Transaction, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party
perturbation.explanation: Changing the purpose of evaluation material use allows the use of this material in any purpose. This contradicts with Section 3, where Evaluation Material should be only used for evaluating a Transaction and for no other purposes
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for any purpose, including purposes beyond evaluating a Transaction, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party; provided , however , that the receiving party may disclose any Evaluation Material (x) if the disclosing party gives its prior express written consent to this disclosure, or (y) to its Representatives who are actively or directly participating in its evaluation of a Transaction or who otherwise need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Transaction (and the receiving party shall take appropriate measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the permitted uses of evaluation material to 'any purpose,' directly contradicting the original text, which limited its use to evaluating a Transaction. The contradicted text also states that the Evaluation Material should be only used for evaluating a Transaction and for no other purposes. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, as the clauses provide conflicting instructions regarding the scope of permitted use of the evaluation material.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, each party agrees that it will not, directly or indirectly (including through its Representatives), solicit for employment any employee of the other party or its subsidiaries who such party comes in contact with, or is introduced to, or is discussed as a key employee, in connection with a Transaction; provided , however , that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prohibit (x) general solicitations of employment conducted through customary means and not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, or (y) the employment of any person who contacts a party on his or her own initiative without any violation of the foregoing provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will not, directly or indirectly (including through its Representatives), solicit for employment any employee of the other party or its subsidiaries who such party comes in contact with, or is introduced to, or is discussed as a key employee, in connection with a Transaction; provided , however , that the foregoing shall  be deemed to prohibit (x) general solicitations of employment conducted through customary means and not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, or (y) the employment of any person who contacts a party on his or her own initiative without any violation of the foregoing provisions.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the time restriction for non-solicitation makes it perpetual, which is contradicting as Section 12 says, the Agreement's term is five years. After 5 years the non-solicitation agreement is deemed to be over.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years from the date first written above.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the non-solicitation agreement to 12 months. The changed text removes this time restriction, making the non-solicitation agreement perpetual. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that the agreement has a term of five years. After five years, according to the contradicted text, the agreement ends. However, the changed text implies the non-solicitation continues indefinitely, thus causing a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder are terminated.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that confidentiality obligations cease upon return of evaluation materials, this directly contradicts the intended lasting effect of the confidentiality terms described in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years from the date first written above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidentiality obligations are terminated upon the return or destruction of evaluation material. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that the agreement lasts for five years, implying continued confidentiality obligations throughout that period. The original text stated that obligations of confidentiality would continue regardless of return of material; now the changed text breaks that continunity. The Perturbation Explanation confirms that this creates an in-text contradiction, satisfying the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party or any of its Representatives and that the non-breaching party would be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages shall be the only remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party or any of its Representatives and that the non-breaching party shall not be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, limited to the money damages
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts original Section 13, which states that injunctive relief and specific performance is available, and that money damages are not the exclusive remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages shall be the only remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party or any of its Representatives and that the non-breaching party shall not be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, limited to the money damages
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages are not the exclusive remedy and equitable relief is available. The changed text explicitly states that money damages are the only remedy, and equitable relief is not available. The contradicted text, which is a repeat of the changed text, now creates a direct contradiction with the original section 13 as described in the perturbation explanation, making the obligations and rights of each party uncertain as to which term should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership;
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that, for a period of one (1) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership;
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the standstill period to 1 month from the date of agreement directly contradicts the agreement's five-year term stated in Section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years from the date first written above.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a 12-month standstill period, while the changed text reduces it to 1 month. This directly contradicts the five-year term of the agreement as stated in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The reduced standstill period fundamentally alters the duration of a key restriction, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the 1 month period directly conflicts with the term of the agreement. The perturbation type is Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
